article_title: Gears of War: E-Day Gets October 6 Launch Date as The Coalition Rebuilds the Series From Scratch on Unreal Engine 5

content_preview.title: Gears of War: E-Day Gets October 6 Launch Date as The Coalition Rebuilds the Series From Scratch on Unreal Engine 5
content_preview.summary: Xbox gave its prequel shooter a firm October 6 release date and an August 6 open beta, with The Coalition saying it rebuilt every asset on Unreal Engine 5.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Microsoft has given *Gears of War: E-Day* a firm launch date. The prequel shooter from developer The Coalition launches October 6, according to [Xbox Wire](https://news.xbox.com/en-us/2026/06/07/gears-of-war-eday-direct-xbox-games-showcase-2026/), the publisher's official news site, which detailed the game during a dedicated showcase. An open beta begins earlier, with [Xbox Wire](https://news.xbox.com/en-us/2026/06/07/gears-of-war-eday-direct-xbox-games-showcase-2026/) confirming ea...

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured News piece (538 words, within the 400-1200 range). Clear Overview / What We Know / What We Don't Know / Analysis sections. Technical depth is appropriate and the developer's from-scratch claim is correctly framed as a studio statement rather than independently verified fact.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 3 source snapshots read from disk and integrity-verified (uncompressed sha256 of each matches manifest.json exactly). source-0.html.gz (Xbox Wire, status 200) confirms: October 6 launch, August 6 open-beta early access for pre-orders, 'Fourteen years before the original Gears of War' with Marcus Fenix and Dominic Santiago, Kate Rayner (Studio Technical Director) quote 'We literally rebuilt everything from scratch,' Matt Searcy (Studio Creative Director) quote 'took advantage of UE5 to bring Gears up to speed with where modern gaming is, without losing what makes it authentic,' the co-op quote 'Battle alone, side by side in two-player split-screen, or rally up to four players online,' and the official platform string 'XBOX Series X|S, XBOX on PC, cloud... Play Anywhere' plus 'Play it day one with Game Pass Ultimate.' source-1.html.gz (Kotaku, status 200) confirms 'canonical prequel set before the events of the first game,' 'launching on Xbox Series X/S and PC,' 'will not be available on PS5,' the ~14-hour 'biggest one ever featured in a Gears game' campaign line, and the 'Xbox's Big Exclusive For 2026' framing. source-2.html.gz (Wikipedia, status 200) confirms the June 7, 2026 reveal at Xbox Games Showcase 2026, the original June 2024 announcement, Unreal Engine 5, Xbox Game Studios publisher, and the 'The Coalition' + 'People Can Fly' developer listing.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Every direct quote appears verbatim in its cited snapshot with correct speakers and titles (Kate Rayner = Studio Technical Director; Matt Searcy = Studio Creative Director). All specifics (Oct 6, Aug 6, 14 years, UE5, platforms) trace to a cited source. No hallucinations detected.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. The Analysis section reads as measured industry observation, attributing the 'tentpole' framing to Kotaku's reporting rather than editorializing.
editor_notes.originality: No prior coverage of Gears of War: E-Day, The Coalition, or this reveal exists in the archive. Genuinely new topic.
editor_notes.platform_string_check: Verified the flagged platform discrepancy. The article cites Xbox Wire's official platform string ('XBOX Series X|S, XBOX on PC, cloud... Play Anywhere' + day-one Game Pass Ultimate) and separately cites Kotaku for the no-PS5 point ('will not be available on PS5'), correctly attributing each. The Xbox Wire snapshot does additionally contain an 'Also available on Steam.' line, which the article omits; the article does NOT overstate or assert Steam availability, so there is no misattribution. The omission is conservative, non-blocking, and not a correction-worthy error.
editor_notes.co_developer_attribution_check: The 'People Can Fly' co-developer detail is attributed to Wikipedia only ('which lists The Coalition and People Can Fly as developers'). The main narrative correctly describes 'developer The Coalition' per Xbox Wire/Kotaku. Attribution is precise.
recommendations: Optional, non-blocking: Xbox Wire's platform string also includes 'Also available on Steam.' The article omits it, which is defensible, but a future update could note Steam availability for completeness.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality, accurate News submission. All integrity checks pass, all three sources personally read and integrity-verified, every quote verbatim with correct speaker attribution, and the sensitive platform/co-developer nuances are handled correctly. APPROVE.
